sit: Your team is killing a penalty in a box formation. The PP moves the puck from the half-wall to the point. What does the box do?
opts: Shift up and toward the puck as a unit to pressure and close lanes
Collapse to the net and let the point player shoot
Break formation and send one player to chase the puck at the point up
Stay completely still and don't move until the puck enters the up

sit: You come off the ice after a shift and notice the other team keeps exploiting weak-side coverage. What do you do?
opts: Tell your linemates what you're seeing so they adjust on the next go
Keep it to yourself since making adjustments is only
Yell at the players currently on the ice to fix the coverage issue
Wait until after the game to mention it so you don't distract

sit: Your D has the puck and is skating up ice with it. You're a forward skating alongside. They're approaching a defender. What's the best support position?
opts: Stay slightly behind and to the outside to be an outlet if they get pressured or need support
Skate ahead of them into the zone to get open for a pass even if that means going offside up
Stay at exactly the same height as them since parallel support is always the best option
Slow down and go back to the defensive zone to make sure someone is covering

sit: You're a D making a breakout pass. Your winger is open on the wall but skating at full speed. Where do you put the pass?
opts: Tape-to-tape — lead them slightly so the puck arrives in stride without them slowing down at all
Right at their feet so they have to stop and collect it before they can continue skating up
Behind them so they have to turn around and come back for the puck
Loft it high in the air so it goes over the forechecker's stick and lands near your winger up ahead

sit: The opposing team is running a neutral zone trap. Your team keeps turning the puck over trying to carry through it. What's the adjustment?
opts: Use D-to-D movement and east-west passes to pull the trap apart before trying to carry through it
Keep trying to carry through since eventually speed and skill will beat the trap if you keep up
Dump the puck every single time and never try to carry through any trap formation
Have your best player try to beat the trap alone since they're the only one skilled enough

sit: Your team is down by one goal entering the third period. The other team plays a tight defensive system. What's the overall approach?
opts: Increase offensive zone time through sustained pressure and cycling — force turnovers in their zone to create chances rather than rushing the attack up the ice
Pull the goalie immediately at the start of the third to get an extra attacker for the full twenty minutes since you need to score and this maximizes your
Keep playing the same way a lucky bounce since changing your approach when you're down usually makes things worse and creates more turnovers
Play ultra-aggressive with no regard for defense since you need a goal and the best defense when trailing is a good offense that puts constant pressure on

sit: You've been winning faceoffs to your forehand all game. The opposing center has adjusted and is now countering your forehand win. How do you re-adjust?
opts: Switch to a different technique — use a backhand tie-up or a draw to a different spot. Varying your faceoff technique prevents the opponent from timing your move consistently.
Keep using the same forehand technique since it's worked all game and one adjustment from the opponent shouldn't change your approach to taking faceoffs
Stop trying to win faceoffs and focus on tying the opposing center up so the wingers can battle for the puck since faceoff wins aren't as important as the puck battle
Let the opposing center win every faceoff for the rest of the game since they've figured out your technique and there's nothing you can do to adjust mid-game against
